sentence_reviews.text: Self-consistency samples multiple reasoning trajectories and aggregates their answers, using diversity to reduce dependence on one brittle chain.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference is modeled as depending on Chain-of-thought & latent reasoning, Recursive partitioning & ensembling and feeding downstream nodes such as Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Test-time compute scaling, Best-of-N & verifier-guided selection.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
